definition: Non-GAAP earnings adjustments systematically widen by 15-25% during commodity inflation cycles, consistently in the flattering direction. Specific mechanisms include: raw material surcharges excluded from core revenue, asymmetric hedge gain/loss treatment, non-recurring inventory write-downs that recur cyclically, and restructuring reclassified as one-time.
evidence: Specific taxonomy of adjustment types identified across commodity inflation episodes
Equity_analyst_01's uncritical use of reported margins identified as the single biggest analytical weakness in pair_1 debate
Investors anchoring on forward non-GAAP P/E systematically overpay during commodity inflation
S&P 500 Industrials: 15% revenue growth, 12% EBITDA growth, but only 6% OCF growth during 2021-23
open_questions: Is SEC scrutiny of non-GAAP adjustments increasing post-2022?
Can quantitative screens systematically detect flattering adjustments?
What is the typical reversion timeline for adjustment ratios?
